item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Without the blood, it was obvious: Miguel Rodriguez, the top man in the Los Diablos cartel, as it was then… 

Before this happened, the cartel were known as Los Diablos. It's never stated explicitly in the scip but they changed their name to Los Ocultos (The Hidden) as a gloating reference to their ability to "hide" their crimes.

Thanks for the feedback, much appreciated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Miguel is the head of Los Diablos but then becomes the head of Los Oculto[Sic] at the end… Am I missing something? Also, that last name is wrong, I didn't correct it because I don't know if you wanted it to be Lo Oculto (The Occult) or Los Ocultos (The Occulted).

Anyways, this was pretty grimdarky and I loved it! Upvoted heartily.

EDIT: After reading the name of the skip in the list again I went ahead and corrected it. Cheers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! Always appreciated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just dropping in because I inexplicably forgot to mention that this was pretty damn great. You had my upvote months ago.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Authors like necroposts { for necropost read: reminder to the world that your scip exists :) }

Ta for the +1

Basically the guy has a wife and kids. The undocumented scip supposedly has something to do with the world's Disappeared. If anything turns up to change has a wife and kids to had a wife and kids, he is to be kept in ignorance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I hope this isn't a necro post, but I feel like I'm missing something with the last paragraph. What does it mean? Maybe I just need it reworded & dumbed down. Lol.

Edit:+1 by the way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not quite. If research into the (as yet undescribed) scip shows that Raul's children/wife are amongst the Disappeared, this info isn't to be made available to him. Maybe they have that info already…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't understand the last sentence. Rodriguez has his children in there?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If the trial happened in Mexico, this just happened to obliterate my suspension of desbelief.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This gave me feels. Like real feels.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Having the ability to make forensic evidence go away would be a perfect ability for a defence lawyer.

It's such a sad story this, how this man wished this ability onto himself, and then he was captured by the very people he was protesting, and forced to be addicted to drugs, and eventually turned into a shell of the man he once was.

+1 for maid outfit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I rather liked this. Then you added the fridge horror at the end, and… dang. I hope they've got some world-class amnestics ready for this poor guy.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Miguel Rodriguez. What a nasty piece of work, even for a Cartel boss.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy crap, this is awesome and super freaking interesting. I love, love, love it. Nice work! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
e: Also, a great take on the lamest Batman villain of all time!

Excellent. Also, how dare you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The crime scene cleaner angle became obvious fairly quickly, but you did some very interesting things with it. The kicker at the end, reminding the reader that it's not just the cartels who have dark secrets, was great. +1

e: Also, a great take on the lamest Batman villain of all time!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks folks. I am however a f*ckwit who missed off the very last paragraph from the end. Now added. Just a little added fridge horror as a "thanks" for reading through to the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The initial description didn't do much for me. But the interviews were absolutely great.

"The blood became salsa became water." you deserve the upvote already just for managing to make me laugh in the middle of a story as dark as this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Horrifically excellent.

item.title: Finally...
item.content:encoded: This is now a thing. May thanks to Shaggydredlocks, Aiden Eldritch and Stormbadger for critique. If this fails, I'm coming for you.
